text.title: Refactoring Pattern Catalog
text.summary: Eight structural transformations with before/after examples: Extract Method, Extract Class, Replace Conditional with Polymorphism, Introduce Parameter Object, Replace Magic Numbers, Decompose Conditional, Rename, Move Method.

rule.statement: Extract a cohesive block of code into a named function when a method exceeds ~20 lines or contains multiple distinct responsibilities.
rule.rationale: Named functions communicate intent; extracted functions are independently testable and reusable.

example.code: # AFTER extract: each function has one responsibility def process_order(order): validate_order(order) totals = calculate_totals(order) process_payment(order.customer, totals.total) send_confirmation(order.customer, totals) return {"order_id": order.id, "total": totals.total}

rule.statement: Extract a group of related fields and methods into a new class when a class has more than one cohesive responsibility.
rule.rationale: Single Responsibility Principle: each class should have one reason to change. Address data belongs in Address, not User.

rule.statement: Replace a type-switch or multi-branch if/elif that dispatches behavior by type with a strategy/polymorphism pattern. Apply only when branches > 4 and each branch is non-trivial.
rule.rationale: Polymorphism extends by adding a new class rather than editing an existing conditional. Reduces cyclomatic complexity at the call site.

rule.statement: Group more than 4 related parameters into a dataclass or NamedTuple. Replace the long parameter list with the new type.
rule.rationale: Long parameter lists are hard to read at call sites and fragile to extend. Parameter objects are self-documenting and extendable without changing signatures.

rule.statement: Replace every numeric or string literal that encodes a business concept with a named constant or enum.
rule.rationale: Magic numbers are undocumented contracts that break when business rules change. Named constants are searchable and document intent.

example.code: if order.total > 100: # What does 100 mean? order.shipping = 0

rule.statement: Extract complex boolean conditions and each branch body into named functions that reveal intent.
rule.rationale: Nested conditions are hard to reason about. Named predicates and action functions make the structure self-documenting.

rule.statement: Use IDE/LSP rename refactoring (not text search-replace) for renaming symbols. Rename when the intent of a variable, function, or class is unclear from its current name.
rule.rationale: LSP rename updates all references including imports. Text replace misses string-based references; grep-check the entire repo for the old name before claiming done.

rule.statement: Move a method to the class it talks to most (Feature Envy smell). A method that only uses data from another class belongs in that class.
rule.rationale: Feature envy creates tight coupling and prevents the moved method from being reused independently.
